title: TC001-Upload valid image via file picker and start extraction
description: Verifies a user can browse-upload a supported image and the app shows a loading skeleton during palette extraction.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/palette
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/palette")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Extracting palette...')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The loading skeleton should be visible while the palette extraction runs.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Copy')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "A copy control should be visible on extracted palette swatches after palette extraction."]}
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The required fixture image for the upload test is not available to the test runner, so I cannot perform the upload or verify the loading skeleton and palette extraction.

Observations:
- The file path testsprite_tests/fixtures/palette-sample.png was not present in the test environment.
- The file input (index 68) is visible on /palette, but upload cannot be performed without the file.

description: Verifies a supported image produces a displayed source image and an extracted swatch grid with color values.

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'HEX')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The extracted palette should display HEX color values after extraction."]} PMID: 0.72%?

title: TC003-Copy a single color value from the extracted palette
description: Users can copy an individual swatch value and receive a success toast confirming the value was copied.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/palette
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/palette")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Copied to clipboard')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The app should show a success toast confirming the value was copied to the clipboard after clicking the swatch."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:

        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

title: TC009-Open palette tool from landing page

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():

title: TC010-Copy All reflects the currently selected output format

title: TC013-Open palette tool from alternate landing CTA
description: Verifies a visitor can use the alternate landing CTA to reach the palette tool and see supported file guidance.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/palette
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/palette")
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link to return to the landing page so the alternate CTA can be exercised.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate to the landing page (/) so I can find and click the alternate/secondary CTA that opens the palette tool.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the alternate/secondary CTA labeled 'Try it now â€” it's free' to open the palette tool.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/palette' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to the palette page after clicking the alternate CTA"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Supported file types')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The upload area should display supported file guidance after opening the palette tool"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

Clicking the secondary CTA on the landing page did not navigate to the palette tool.

Observations:
- The 'Try it now â€” it's free' CTA on the landing page was clicked twice but the page did not navigate and the landing page remained visible.
- The palette page (showing 'PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP Â· Max 10 MB' guidance) is reachable by direct navigation, but the alternate CTA does not trigger navigation to it.

title: TC015-Show error state for corrupted image
description: Verifies a corrupted or broken image file results in a visible error state indicating the image could not be processed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/palette
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/palette")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'The image could not be processed.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The error state should be visible because the uploaded image is corrupted and could not be processed.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'No palette could be extracted from this image.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The palette swatches should not be displayed because the corrupted image could not be processed and no colors were extracted."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
